metadata.name: autoXRD
metadata.summary: A package for automated XRD analysis

functions.name: autoXRD.cnn.train_model
documentation:
Args:
    x_train: numpy array of simulated xrd spectra
    y_train: one-hot encoded vectors associated with reference phase indices
    n_phases: number of reference phases considered
    fmodel: filename to save trained model to
    n_dense: number of nodes comprising the two hidden layers in the neural network
    dropout_rate: fraction of connections excluded between the hidden layers during training
Returns:
    model: trained and compiled tensorflow.keras.Model object

functions.name: autoXRD.cnn.test_model
documentation:
Args:
    model: trained tensorflow.keras.Model object
    x_test: feature matrix containing xrd spectra
    y_test: one-hot encoded vectors associated with
        the reference phases

classes.name: autoXRD.cnn.CustomDropout

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.CustomDropout.call
documentation: Always apply dropout
Used to apply dropout during training *and* inference

classes.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    xrd: a numpy array containing xrd spectra categorized by
        their associated reference phase.
        The shape of the array should be NxMx4501x1 where:
        N = the number of reference phases,
        M = the number of augmented spectra per reference phase,
        4501 = intensities as a function of 2-theta
        (spanning from 10 to 80 degrees by default)
    testing_fraction: fraction of data (xrd patterns) to reserve for testing.
        By default, all spectra will be used for training.

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp.phase_indices
documentation:
List of indices to keep track of xrd spectra such that
    each index is associated with a reference phase.

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp.x
documentation: Feature matrix (array of intensities used for training)

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp.y
documentation:
Target property to predict (one-hot encoded vectors associated
with the reference phases)

methods.name: autoXRD.cnn.DataSetUp.split_training_testing
documentation:
Training and testing data will be split according
to self.testing_fraction

Returns:
    x_train, x_test: features matrices (xrd spectra) to be
        used for training and testing
    y_train, t_test: target properties (one-hot encoded phase indices)
        to be used for training and testing

Class used to train a convolutional neural network on a given
set of X-ray diffraction spectra to perform phase identification.

classes.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.strain_shifts.StrainGen

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.strain_shifts.StrainGen.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    struc: pymatgen structure object
    max_strain: maximum allowed change in the magnitude
        of the strain tensor components

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.strain_shifts.StrainGen.calc_std_dev
documentation:
calculate standard deviation based on angle (two theta) and domain size (tau)
Args:
    two_theta: angle in two theta space
    tau: domain size in nm
Returns:
    standard deviation for gaussian kernel

Class used to apply stochastic, symmetry-preserving sets of
strain to a pymatgen structure object.

classes.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.uniform_shifts.ShiftGen

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.uniform_shifts.ShiftGen.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    struc: structure to simulate augmented xrd spectra from
    min_domain_size: smallest domain size (in nm) to be sampled,
        leading to the broadest peaks
    max_domain_size: largest domain size (in nm) to be sampled,
        leading to the most narrow peaks

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.uniform_shifts.ShiftGen.calc_std_dev
documentation:
calculate standard deviation based on angle (two theta) and domain size (tau)
Args:
    two_theta: angle in two theta space
    tau: domain size in nm
Returns:
    standard deviation for gaussian kernel

Class used to simulate xrd spectra with broad peaks
    that are associated with small domain size

classes.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.SpectraGenerator

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.SpectraGenerator.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    reference_dir: path to directory containing
        CIFs associated with the reference phases

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.SpectraGenerator.augment
documentation:
For a given phase, produce a list of augmented XRD spectra.
By default, 50 spectra are generated per artifact, including
peak shifts (strain), peak intensity change (texture), and
peak broadening (small domain size).

Args:
    phase_info: a list containing the pymatgen structure object
        and filename of that structure respectively.
Returns:
    patterns: augmented XRD spectra
    filename: filename of the reference phase

Class used to generate augmented xrd spectra
for all reference phases

functions.name: autoXRD.visualizer.scale_values
documentation: Scale a list of values to a new given range [new_min, new_max].

classes.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    spectrum_fname: name of file containing the
        xrd spectrum (in xy format)
    reference_dir: path to directory containing the
        reference phases (CIF files)

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.get_reduced_pattern
documentation:
Subtract a phase that has already been identified from a given XRD spectrum.
If all phases have already been identified, halt the iteration.

Args:
    predicted_cmpd: phase that has been identified
    orig_y: measured spectrum including the phase the above phase
    last_normalization: normalization factor used to scale the previously stripped
        spectrum to 100 (required by the CNN). This is necessary to determine the
        magnitudes of intensities relative to the initially measured pattern.
    cutoff: the % cutoff used to halt the phase ID iteration. If all intensities are
        below this value in terms of the originally measured maximum intensity, then
        the code assumes that all phases have been identified.
Returns:
    stripped_y: new spectrum obtained by subtrating the peaks of the identified phase
    new_normalization: scaling factor used to ensure the maximum intensity is equal to 100
    Or
    If intensities fall below the cutoff, preserve orig_y and return Nonetype
        the for new_normalization constant.

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.scale_spectrum
documentation:
Scale the magnitude of a calculated spectrum associated with an identified
phase so that its peaks match with those of the measured spectrum being classified.

Args:
    pred_y: spectrum calculated from the identified phase after fitting
        has been performed along the x-axis using DTW
    obs_y: observed (experimental) spectrum containing all peaks
Returns:
    scaled_spectrum: spectrum associated with the reference phase after scaling
        has been performed to match the peaks in the measured pattern.

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.strip_spectrum
documentation:
Subtract one spectrum from another. Note that when subtraction produces
negative intensities, those values are re-normalized to zero. This way,
the CNN can handle the spectrum reliably.

Args:
    warped_spectrum: spectrum associated with the identified phase
    orig_y: original (measured) spectrum
Returns:
    fixed_y: resulting spectrum from the subtraction of warped_spectrum
        from orig_y

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.generate_pattern
documentation:
Calculate the XRD spectrum of a given compound.

Args:
    cmpd: filename of the structure file to calculate the spectrum for
Returns:
    all_I: list of intensities as a function of two-theta

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.convert_angle
documentation: Convert two-theta into Cu K-alpha radiation.

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.formatted_spectrum
documentation:
Cleans up a measured spectrum and format it such that it
is directly readable by the CNN.

Args:
    spectrum_name: filename of the spectrum that is being considered
Returns:
    ys: Processed XRD spectrum in 4501x1 form.

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.calc_std_dev
documentation:
calculate standard deviation based on angle (two theta) and domain size (tau)
Args:
    two_theta: angle in two theta space
    tau: domain size in nm
Returns:
    standard deviation for gaussian kernel

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.smooth_spectrum
documentation:
Process and remove noise from the spectrum.

Args:
    spectrum: list of intensities as a function of 2-theta
    n: parameters used to control smooth. Larger n means greater smoothing.
        20 is typically a good number such that noise is reduced while
        still retaining minor diffraction peaks.
Returns:
    smoothed_ys: processed spectrum after noise removal

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.scaled_patterns
documentation:
Get line profiles of predicted phases that are scaled
to match with peaks in the measured spectrum

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.get_stick_pattern
documentation:
Returns:
    all_I: list of intensities as a function of two-theta

methods.name: autoXRD.visualizer.SpectrumPlotter.scale_line_profile
documentation:
Identify the scaling factor that minimizes the differences between a line
profile and any associated peaks in a measured XRD spectrum.

Args:
    angles: a list of diffraction angles
    intensities: a list of peak intensities
Returns:
    best_scale: a float ranging from 0.05 to 1.0 that has been optimized
        to ensure maximal overlap between the line profile and the peaks
        in the measured spectrum.

Class used to plot and compare:
(i) measured xrd spectra
(ii) line profiles of identified phases

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.calc_std_dev
documentation:
calculate standard deviation based on angle (two theta) and domain size (tau)
Args:
    two_theta: angle in two theta space
    tau: domain size in nm
Returns:
    standard deviation for gaussian kernel

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.smooth_spectrum
documentation:
Process and remove noise from the spectrum.

Args:
    spectrum: list of intensities as a function of 2-theta
    n: parameters used to control smooth. Larger n means greater smoothing.
        20 is typically a good number such that noise is reduced while
        still retaining minor diffraction peaks.
Returns:
    smoothed_ys: processed spectrum after noise removal

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.strip_spectrum
documentation:
Subtract one spectrum from another. Note that when subtraction produces
negative intensities, those values are re-normalized to zero. This way,
the CNN can handle the spectrum reliably.

Args:
    warped_spectrum: spectrum associated with the identified phase
    orig_y: original (measured) spectrum
Returns:
    fixed_y: resulting spectrum from the subtraction of warped_spectrum
        from orig_y

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.scale_spectrum
documentation:
Scale the magnitude of a calculated spectrum associated with an identified
phase so that its peaks match with those of the measured spectrum being classified.

Args:
    pred_y: spectrum calculated from the identified phase after fitting
        has been performed along the x-axis using DTW
    obs_y: observed (experimental) spectrum containing all peaks
Returns:
    scaled_spectrum: spectrum associated with the reference phase after scaling
        has been performed to match the peaks in the measured pattern.

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.get_reduced_pattern
documentation:
Subtract y1 from y2 using dynamic time warping (DTW) and return the new spectrum.
Returns:
    stripped_y: new spectrum obtained by subtrating the peaks of the identified phase

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.round_dict_values
documentation:
Used to round off coefficients
of highly complex formulae.

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.balance_oxidation_states
documentation:
Note: this is *not* an exhaustive oxidation state solver.
Rather, it will find if there exists at least one solution
that satisfies charge balance given the possible oxidation
states. This method is fast and suitable for the current
application; however, caution should be used if one
implements it outside of XRD-AutoAnalyzer.

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.oxi_filter
documentation:
Removes any reference compounds that have
unusual oxidation states.

Args:
    cif_dir: directory containing CIFs

functions.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.write_cifs
documentation:
Write structures to CIF files

Args:
    strucs: list of pymatgen Structure objects
    dir: path to directory where CIF files will be written

classes.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter

methods.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    cif_directory: path to directory containing
        the CIF files to be considered as
        possible reference phases

methods.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter.stoichiometric_info
documentation:
Filter strucures to include only those which do not have
fraction occupancies and are ordered. For those phases, tabulate
the measurement conditions of the associated CIFs.

Returns:
    stoich_strucs: a list of ordered pymatgen Structure objects
    temps: temperatures that each were measured at
    dates: dates the measurements were reported

methods.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter.parse_measurement_conditions
documentation:
Parse the temperature and date from a CIF file

Args:
    filename: filename of CIF to be parsed
Returns:
    temp: temperature at which measurement was conducted
    date: date which measurement was reported

methods.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter.unique_struc_info
documentation:
Create distinct lists of Structure objects where each
list is associated with a unique strucural prototype

Returns:
    grouped_strucs: a list of sub-lists containing pymatgen
        Structure objects organize by the strucural prototype
    grouped_temps and grouped_dates: similarly grouped temperatures and dates
        associated with the corresponding measurements

methods.name: autoXRD.tabulate_cifs.StructureFilter.filtered_refs
documentation:
For each list of strucures associated with a strucural prototype,
choose that which was measured under (or nearest to) ambient conditions
and which was reported most recently. Priority is given to the former.

Returns:
    filtered_cmpds: a list of unique pymatgen Structure objects

Class used to parse a list of CIFs and choose unique,
stoichiometric reference phases that were measured
under (or nearest to) ambient conditions.

classes.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.intensity_changes.TextureGen

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.intensity_changes.TextureGen.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    struc: pymatgen structure object from which xrd
        spectra are simulated
    max_texture: maximum strength of texture applied.
        For example, max_texture=0.6 implies peaks will be
        scaled by as much as +/- 60% of their original
        intensities.

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.intensity_changes.TextureGen.map_interval
documentation:
Maps a value (v) from the interval [0, 1] to
    a new interval [1 - max_texture, 1]

methods.name: autoXRD.spectrum_generation.intensity_changes.TextureGen.calc_std_dev
documentation:
calculate standard deviation based on angle (two theta) and domain size (tau)
Args:
    two_theta: angle in two theta space
    tau: domain size in nm
Returns:
    standard deviation for gaussian kernel

Class used to simulate diffraction patterns with
    peak intensities scale according to texture along
    stochastically chosen crystallographic directions.

documentation:
Failed to index ``autoXRD/spectrum_analysis/__init__.py`` due to ``Syntax Error @ 1:1.
tokenizer error: no matching outer block for dedent

from scipy.signal import find_peaks, filtfilt, resample
^``

classes.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen

methods.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    reference_directory: path to directory containing
        the stoichiometric reference phases (CIFs)

methods.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen.soluble_pairs
documentation:
Returns:
    matching_pairs: list of tuples containing pairs of soluble compounds
        (denoted by their filenames)

methods.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen.are_soluble
documentation:
Predict whether a pair of compounds are soluble with one another.

Args:
    pair_info: a tuple containing containing two compounds
        denoted by their filenames
Returns:
    The pair of compounds, if they are soluble.
    Otherwise, Nonetype is returned.

methods.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen.generate_solid_solns
documentation:
From a given pair of soluble compounds, interpolate a list
of solid solutions.

Args:
    pair: a list containing two compounds denoated by
        their filenames
    num_solns: number of solid solutions to interpolate
        between each pair of compounds
Returns:
    interp_structs: a list of interpolated solids solutions
        (pymatgen Structure objects)

methods.name: autoXRD.solid_solns.SolidSolnsGen.all_solid_solns
documentation:
Returns:
    all_solid_solns: a list of interpolated pymatgen Structure
        objects associated with hypothetical solid solutions

Class used to generate hypothetical solid solutions by interpolating
from a list of stoichiometric reference phases

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.max_phases
documentation: default: a maximum 4 phases in each mixture

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.cutoff_intensity
documentation: default: ID all peaks with I >= 5% maximum spectrum intensity

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: Minimum confidence included in predictions

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.wavelength
documentation: default: spectra was measured using Cu K_alpha radiation

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.unknown_threshold
documentation: default: raise warning when peaks with >= 25% intensity are unknown

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.show_reduced
documentation: Whether to plot reduced spectrum (after subtraction of known phases)

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.inc_pdf
documentation: Whether to include PDF analysis (requires trained model first)

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.parallel
documentation: Run phase analysis in parallel across available CPUs

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.raw
documentation: Whether to show the raw spectrum or its denoised product

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.show_indiv
documentation: Whether to show individual predictions from XRD and PDF

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.final_conf
documentation: For final (merged) predictions

variables.name: PDF_Example.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: For individual (XRD/PDF) predictions

variables.name: Novel-Space.visualize.scale_factors
documentation: Not known yet

variables.name: Novel-Space.visualize.final_spectrum
documentation: Not known yet

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.max_texture
documentation: default: texture associated with up to +/- 50% changes in peak intensities

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.max_strain
documentation: default: up to +/- 3% strain

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.max_shift
documentation: default: up to +/- 0.5 degrees shift in two-theta

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.impur_amt
documentation: Max amount of impurity phases to include (%)

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.num_spectra
documentation: Number of spectra to simulate per phase

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_pdf_model.separate
documentation: If False: apply all artifacts simultaneously

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.max_phases
documentation: default: a maximum 4 phases in each mixture

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.cutoff_intensity
documentation: default: ID all peaks with I >= 5% maximum spectrum intensity

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: Minimum confidence included in predictions

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.wavelength
documentation: default: spectra was measured using Cu K_alpha radiation

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.unknown_threshold
documentation: default: raise warning when peaks with >= 25% intensity are unknown

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.show_reduced
documentation: Whether to plot reduced spectrum (after subtraction of known phases)

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.inc_pdf
documentation: Whether to include PDF analysis (requires trained model first)

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.parallel
documentation: Run phase analysis in parallel across available CPUs

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.raw
documentation: Whether to show the raw spectrum or its denoised product

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.show_indiv
documentation: Whether to show individual predictions from XRD and PDF

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.final_conf

variables.name: Novel-Space.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: For individual (XRD/PDF) predictions

classes.name: autoXRD.quantifier.QuantAnalysis

methods.name: autoXRD.quantifier.QuantAnalysis.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    spectrum_fname: name of file containing the
        xrd spectrum (in xy format)
    reference_dir: path to directory containing the
        reference phases (CIF files)

methods.name: autoXRD.quantifier.QuantAnalysis.get_reduced_pattern
documentation:
Subtract a phase that has already been identified from a given XRD spectrum.
If all phases have already been identified, halt the iteration.

Args:
    predicted_cmpd: phase that has been identified
    orig_y: measured spectrum including the phase the above phase
    last_normalization: normalization factor used to scale the previously stripped
        spectrum to 100 (required by the CNN). This is necessary to determine the
        magnitudes of intensities relative to the initially measured pattern.
    cutoff: the % cutoff used to halt the phase ID iteration. If all intensities are
        below this value in terms of the originally measured maximum intensity, then
        the code assumes that all phases have been identified.
Returns:
    stripped_y: new spectrum obtained by subtrating the peaks of the identified phase
    new_normalization: scaling factor used to ensure the maximum intensity is equal to 100
    Or
    If intensities fall below the cutoff, preserve orig_y and return Nonetype
        the for new_normalization constant.

documentation: Get the number from a float or tuple of floats.

functions.name: autoXRD.dara.read_phase_name_from_str
documentation:
Get the phase name from the str file path.

Example of str:
PHASE=BaSnO3 // generated from pymatgen
FORMULA=BaSnO3 //
Lattice=Cubic HermannMauguin=P4/m-32/m Setting=1 SpacegroupNo=221 //
PARAM=A=0.41168_0.40756^0.41580 //
RP=4 PARAM=k1=0_0^1 k2=0 PARAM=B1=0_0^0.01 PARAM=GEWICHT=0_0 //
GOAL:BaSnO3=GEWICHT //
GOAL=GrainSize(1,1,1) //
E=BA+2 Wyckoff=b x=0.500000 y=0.500000 z=0.500000 TDS=0.010000
E=SN+4 Wyckoff=a x=0.000000 y=0.000000 z=0.000000 TDS=0.010000
E=O-2 Wyckoff=d x=0.000000 y=0.000000 z=0.500000 TDS=0.010000

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.max_texture
documentation: default: texture associated with up to +/- 50% changes in peak intensities

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.max_strain
documentation: default: up to +/- 3% strain

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.max_shift
documentation: default: up to +/- 0.5 degrees shift in two-theta

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.impur_amt
documentation: Max amount of impurity phases to include (%)

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.num_spectra
documentation: Number of spectra to simulate per phase

variables.name: Novel-Space.construct_xrd_model.separate
documentation: If False: apply all artifacts simultaneously

variables.name: Example.visualize.scale_factors
documentation: Not known yet

variables.name: Example.visualize.final_spectrum
documentation: Not known yet

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.max_phases
documentation: default: a maximum 4 phases in each mixture

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.cutoff_intensity
documentation: default: ID all peaks with I >= 5% maximum spectrum intensity

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: Minimum confidence included in predictions

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.wavelength
documentation: default: spectra was measured using Cu K_alpha radiation

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.unknown_threshold
documentation: default: raise warning when peaks with >= 25% intensity are unknown

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.show_reduced
documentation: Whether to plot reduced spectrum (after subtraction of known phases)

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.inc_pdf
documentation: Whether to include PDF analysis (requires trained model first)

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.parallel
documentation: Run phase analysis in parallel across available CPUs

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.raw
documentation: Whether to show the raw spectrum or its denoised product

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.show_indiv
documentation: Whether to show individual predictions from XRD and PDF

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.final_conf
documentation: For final (merged) predictions

variables.name: Example.run_CNN.min_conf
documentation: For individual (XRD/PDF) predictions
